Class ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>

java.lang.Object
org.key_project.prover.engine.impl.ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof,Goal>
All Implemented Interfaces:
ProofSearchInformation<Proof,Goal>
public class ApplyStrategyInfo<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>> extends Object implements ProofSearchInformation<Proof,Goal>

A container class representing the final result of a proof strategy application.

This implementation of ProofSearchInformation provides detailed statistical information about the proof process, such as the number of applied rules, time taken, and the number of closed goals. It also retains information about unresolved goals or exceptions encountered during the strategy execution.

Instances of this class are designed to be immutable and are returned to the caller of the strategy application to provide insights or for user interaction.

Key features include:

  • Tracking the time taken for the strategy execution.
  • Recording the number of applied rule applications and closed goals.
  • Providing details of unresolved goals and any errors encountered.
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • ApplyStrategyInfo

      public ApplyStrategyInfo(String message, Proof proof, @Nullable Throwable error, @Nullable Goal nonCloseableGoal, long timeInMillis, int appliedRuleAppsCount, int nrClosedGoals)
      Constructs an ApplyStrategyInfo object with all necessary details of the strategy execution.
      Parameters:
      message - the message explaining the reason for the termination of strategy execution
      proof - the proof object associated with the strategy execution
      error - the exception encountered during execution, or null if no error occurred
      nonCloseableGoal - the non-closeable goal, or null if all goals were closed
      timeInMillis - the total execution time in milliseconds
      appliedRuleAppsCount - the number of applied rule applications
      nrClosedGoals - the number of successfully closed goals

  • Method Details

    • reason

      public String reason()

      Retrieves the explanation or reason wjy the proof search (strategy execution) finished.

      This message provides context for why the strategy terminated, and it can be used for logging or user feedback purposes. Possible reasons are for example (not exhaustive): proof could be closed, timeout, maximal number of rule applications reached or an exception was thrown.

      Specified by:
      reason in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      a string describing the reason for applying the strategy

    • nonCloseableGoal

      public @Nullable Goal nonCloseableGoal()

      Retrieves one of non-closeable proof goals, if one exists.

      A non-closeable goal represents a proof goal that could not be resolved or closed by the applied strategy. This can occur when the automated reasoning process encounters conditions requiring external input or manual intervention.

      Specified by:
      nonCloseableGoal in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      the unresolved goal of type G, or null if all goals were closed

    • isError

      @EnsuresNonNullIf(expression="error", result=true) public boolean isError()

      Indicates whether an error occurred during the strategy execution.

      An error might occur due to unexpected conditions, such as invalid inputs, unsupported proof configurations, or system failures during the reasoning process.

      Specified by:
      isError in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      true if an error occurred, otherwise false

    • getException

      public @Nullable Throwable getException()

      Retrieves the exception that occurred during strategy execution, if any.

      This method provides access to the specific Throwable encountered during the proof strategy application, which can be used for debugging or error handling purposes.

      Specified by:
      getException in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      the exception encountered during execution, or null if no error occurred

    • getTime

      public long getTime()

      Returns the total time taken for the strategy application, in milliseconds.

      This metric allows for performance evaluation and optimization of proof strategies.

      Specified by:
      getTime in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      the execution time in milliseconds

    • getNumberOfClosedGoals

      public int getNumberOfClosedGoals()

      Retrieves the number of proof goals that were successfully closed during the strategy execution.

      A closed goal is one that was fully resolved and requires no further reasoning.

      Specified by:
      getNumberOfClosedGoals in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      the number of closed goals

    • getNumberOfAppliedRuleApps

      public int getNumberOfAppliedRuleApps()

      Retrieves the number of rule applications performed during the strategy execution.

      This statistic provides insight into the complexity and effort involved in resolving the proof.

      Specified by:
      getNumberOfAppliedRuleApps in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      the number of rule applications performed

    • getProof

      public Proof getProof()

      Returns the proof object associated with this strategy application.

      The proof object represents the state of the proof at the conclusion of the strategy execution.

      Specified by:
      getProof in interface ProofSearchInformation<Proof extends ProofObject<Goal>, Goal extends @Nullable ProofGoal<Goal>>
      Returns:
      the proof object of type P

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Provides a string representation of this ApplyStrategyInfo object, including all key details such as message, error status, applied rules, execution time, and closed goals.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string summarizing the state of this ApplyStrategyInfo object
